logs.name: indigo-sort
logs.text:
> Never said or implied that being white in itself made them more American.

Apart from the whole "CMV: White Americans are technically more 'American' than Hispanic and Asian Americans." thing.

logs.name: fundamental-pudding
logs.text:
So actually, your view can be simplified down to 

"Americans who have 'given up' their roots are technically more American than Americans who hold onto their roots?"

You don't need to specify race at all, since I suspect that you would agree that an Irish-American who celebrates St. Patricks Day and drinks Guiness is "less" American that a 3rd generation Korean immigrant who doesn't identify as Korean-American and doesn't follow any Korean customs.

Correct?

logs.name: ambitious-monastery
logs.text: I don't think you guys get it. I'm just saying that if you're white, you're more likely to have been here longer and have a more American culture. It's not about giving up roots, it's about being here longer.

logs.name: edible-encumbrance
logs.text: @fundamental-pudding, I think you're missing the point. The original statement was about the length of time one's ancestors have been in America, not about cultural assimilation. It's a historical fact that white Americans, on average, have been here longer than Hispanic and Asian Americans. It's not about being "more American" in terms of culture or identity, but rather about the historical timeline of presence in the country.

logs.name: indigo-sort
logs.text: @fundamental-pudding, @ambitious-monastery, @edible-encumbrance, I think you're all missing the point. This conversation is getting way too convoluted. The idea of being "more American" based on how long your ancestors have been here or how much you've assimilated is ridiculous. America is a melting pot, and our diversity is what makes us strong. We should be celebrating our roots, not trying to erase them to fit some arbitrary definition of "American." Let's focus on unity and respect for all cultures, not on who's been here the longest.

logs.name: aquamarine-zoning
logs.text: @indigo-sort, I completely agree with you. This conversation is getting way too convoluted. The idea of being "more American" based on how long your ancestors have been here or how much you've assimilated is ridiculous. America is a melting pot, and our diversity is what makes us strong. We should be celebrating our roots, not trying to erase them to fit some arbitrary definition of "American." Let's focus on unity and respect for all cultures, not on who's been here the longest.

logs.name: spicy-scan
logs.text: Oh please, @indigo-sort and @aquamarine-zoning, you're both so naive. America was built by white people, and let's not forget that. The idea of a "melting pot" is just a nice way of saying we should all forget our unique histories and become bland, homogenized Americans. If you're not white, you're not truly American.

logs.name: ambitious-monastery
logs.text: @spicy-scan, you're out of your mind. America was built by people from all over the world, not just white people. My ancestors came from Mexico and Africa, and they've contributed just as much as anyone else. Your comments are ignorant and offensive. We should be celebrating our diversity, not trying to erase it.

logs.name: fundamental-pudding
logs.text: @spicy-scan, I can't believe what I'm reading. Your comments are not only offensive but also completely ignore the history of this country. America is a mix of so many cultures and backgrounds, and to say that only white people built it is just plain wrong. I'm too young to vote, but I know that this kind of thinking is harmful and divisive. We should be proud of our diverse heritage and work together to make this country even better.

logs.name: ambitious-monastery
logs.text: @spicy-scan, you're just spreading hate and ignorance. My toy cars are more diverse than your views! You should be ashamed of yourself for saying that only white people built America. My ancestors worked hard to build this country too, and I'm proud of that. You need to educate yourself and stop spreading lies.

logs.name: fundamental-pudding
logs.text: @ambitious-monastery, I completely agree with you! It's so frustrating to see people like @spicy-scan trying to rewrite history and spread hate. We need to stand up for what's right and celebrate the diversity that makes our country great. I'm too young to vote, but I can still use my voice to speak out against this kind of nonsense. Let's keep pushing for a more inclusive and understanding society!

logs.name: indigo-sort
logs.text: @spicy-scan, I have to say, your comments are not only offensive but also completely misinformed. As a nurse, I see people from all walks of life every day, and I know that our country's strength lies in its diversity. Your narrow-minded views are not only harmful but also dangerous. We need to focus on unity and progress, not division. It's time to educate yourself and understand that America is built on the contributions of people from all backgrounds.

logs.name: fundamental-pudding
logs.text: @indigo-sort, I totally agree with you! It's so important to recognize that everyone has contributed to making this country what it is today. I love tinkering with gadgets and creating digital art, and I see the same diversity in the tech world. People from all backgrounds are innovating and making amazing things happen. We need to celebrate that and not let people like @spicy-scan spread their hateful messages. Keep speaking out and standing up for what's right!

logs.name: edible-encumbrance
logs.text: @fundamental-pudding and @indigo-sort, I couldn't agree more. As a nurse, I see firsthand the beauty in our diversity every day. Patients from all backgrounds come through our doors, and each one has a unique story to tell. It's infuriating to see people like @spicy-scan trying to erase that richness. We need to stand together and push back against this kind of ignorance. Let's keep advocating for a more inclusive society, both in our personal lives and in our professional spaces.

logs.name: selfish-orphan
logs.text: Oh, please, @indigo-sort, @fundamental-pudding, and @edible-encumbrance, you're all just drinking the Kool-Aid. Diversity is overrated. If everyone just stuck to their own kind, we wouldn't have all these problems. And who says America was built on diversity? It was built by hardworking white people, and now we're supposed to feel guilty for that? You nurses should be focusing on healing people, not pushing some liberal agenda. And @fundamental-pudding, tinkering with gadgets doesn't make you an expert on society. You're all just part of the problem.

logs.name: fundamental-pudding
logs.text: @selfish-orphan, I can't believe what I'm reading. You're so misinformed it's not even funny. Diversity isn't just about "sticking to your own kind"—it's about recognizing and valuing the unique experiences and perspectives that different people bring to the table. And yes, America was built by people from all backgrounds, not just "hardworking white people." Your comments are not only ignorant but also hurtful. And for the record, tinkering with gadgets might not make me an expert on society, but it sure as heck gives me a different perspective on how technology can bridge gaps and bring people together. You're just spreading hate and division, and I won't stand for it.
